claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 2 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have three children I'd like to claim for.

There's Alex who's 3 years old, Blake who's 8, and Charlie who's 2. They all live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know about Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2), and that they all live at home with you.

I have a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

Are you responsible for these children? By "responsible," I mean you're the one who pays for things like their food, clothes, and accommodation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all three of them - I pay for their food, clothes, where we live, everything they need really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That's great to know.

Now, could you tell me a bit about your own situation? Specifically:

Are you aged 16 or over, and do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm over 16 and I live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you! Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Regarding your children's education or training situation:

- Is Blake (8) in full-time education?
- For Alex (3) and Charlie (2), are they at home with you or in any form of childcare/nursery?

Also, are any of your children receiving any other government benefits in their own right, or are any of them in local authority care or hospital?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Blake is in primary school, so yes, he's in full-time education. Alex and Charlie are at home with me most of the time - Alex goes to nursery a couple of days a week, but that's it.

No, none of them are receiving any benefits in their own right, and none of them are in care or hospital - they're all healthy and living at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me just check the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information about your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information! Based on what you've told me and the official eligibility rules, I have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You are over 16 and live in the UK (Manchester)
- You have three children: Alex (3), Blake (8), and Charlie (2)
- All three children live with you at home
- You are responsible for all three children (paying for their food, clothes, accommodation, etc.)
- None of the children are in local authority care or hospital
- None of the children are receiving benefits in their own right
- Blake is in full-time education; Alex and Charlie are under 16

Based on this information, I'm pleased to tell you that you appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for all three of your children**. Here's the breakdown:

### Alex (age 3) ✅ Eligible
Alex is under 16, lives with you, and you are responsible for their care. Alex is not in local authority care or hospital and doesn't receive any benefits in their own right.

### Blake (age 8) ✅ Eligible
Blake is under 16, lives with you, and you are responsible for their care. Blake is in full-time education at primary school, is not in local authority care or hospital, and doesn't receive any benefits in their own right.

### Charlie (age 2) ✅ Eligible
Charlie is under 16, lives with you, and you are responsible for their care. Charlie is not in local authority care or hospital and doesn't receive any benefits in their own right.

### Summary
As someone who is over 16, living in the UK, and responsible for three children who all live with you, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. Is there anything else you'd like to know about the application process or Child Benefit in general?

Blake (age 8): Eligible. Blake is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Blake (paying for food, clothes, and accommodation). Blake is in full-time education at primary school. Blake is not in local authority care or hospital, and is not receiving any benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria are met.

Charlie (age 2): Eligible. Charlie is under 16, lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Charlie (paying for food, clothes, and accommodation). Charlie is not in local authority care or hospital, and is not receiving any benefits in their own right. All eligibility criteria are met.
